expectations.evidence: Three individual review files produced (cloud-migration, sap-on-cloud, sovereign-cloud) plus portfolio-review-summary.json with solutions_reviewed: 3.

expectations.text: REV-02: Each assessment has three perspective scores
expectations.evidence: Each review file contains stakeholder_reviews with exactly 3 entries: reviewer (scores 82/85/78), provider_sa (scores 76/80/70), client_sa (scores 75/73/73). All include numeric scores and per-criteria breakdowns.

Each review's synthesis section contains critical_improvements (empty arrays — none needed), high_improvements (3-4 items per solution), and optional_improvements (2-3 items per solution). Priority levels are structurally present and correctly categorized.

expectations.text: REV-04: Recommendations are specific and actionable (reference specific fields/values)
expectations.evidence: Recommendations reference specific fields (cost_model.assumptions, implementation[2].duration_weeks, pricing.medium.scope), specific values (28.8% margin, 22 SA days, 8-week migration phase, 4-week exit clause), and specific standards (BSI-C5 Typ 1 vs Typ 2, IEC 62351, OPC-UA, NIS2 April 2026 deadline).

expectations.text: REV-05: Portfolio-level summary identifies cross-solution patterns
expectations.evidence: portfolio-review-summary.json contains cross_solution_patterns with 4 shared_strengths, 5 shared_weaknesses (OT/IT blind spot, no competitive positioning, fixed phase durations, client SA lowest scores, Small tier margins), and 2 inconsistencies (exit clause presence, NIS2 coverage). Each pattern names affected solutions and includes a recommendation.
